picks.rationale: The Cleveland Cavaliers are in a strong position, holding a 3-2 series lead and playing at home, where they boast an impressive +8.4 net rating this postseason. The Detroit Pistons are severely hampered by injuries, with key offensive players Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert, and Kevin Huerter all questionable, significantly impacting their scoring ability. Cleveland has won their last three games against Detroit and the line movement towards the Cavaliers suggests sharp money is backing them to close out the series.

Donovan Mitchell is active and has been the Cavaliers' leading scorer, averaging 27.9 points per game in the regular season and demonstrating his scoring prowess with 43 points in Game 4. With the Pistons facing elimination and potentially missing key defenders due to injuries, Mitchell is expected to be aggressive in a pivotal home game. His ability to take over offensively makes him a strong candidate to exceed this point total.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Minnesota Timberwolves
picks.rationale: The San Antonio Spurs lead the series 3-2 and are coming off a dominant 126-97 victory in Game 5, showcasing their ability to control the game. San Antonio has an excellent record against the spread, covering in 8 of their last 10 road games and 15 of their last 20 overall. The line has moved slightly in favor of the Spurs, and predictive models indicate a high probability (72%) of a Spurs win, with a 53% chance of covering the spread.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Minnesota Timberwolves
picks.rationale: Victor Wembanyama is active and has been a dominant force for the Spurs, particularly in Game 5 where he recorded 27 points. He is averaging 20.4 points per game and has consistently shown the ability to impact the game offensively. With the Spurs looking to close out the series, Wembanyama will be a focal point of their attack, and the Timberwolves have struggled to contain his presence in the paint.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Minnesota Timberwolves
picks.rationale: Despite the Spurs' strong defense, recent head-to-head matchups between these two teams have trended towards higher scoring, with the Over hitting in 4 of the Timberwolves' last 5 games. Game 5 of this series saw a total of 223 points, and Game 1 reached 228 points. With Minnesota facing elimination at home, they are likely to push the pace and increase offensive aggression, contributing to a game that exceeds the projected total.

The Atlanta Braves are performing exceptionally well, holding the best record in baseball at 30-14 and winning four of their last five games, with their pitching staff allowing only nine runs in that span. Braves ace Spencer Strider (1-0, 2.89 ERA) is coming off a dominant outing, throwing six scoreless innings in his last start. In contrast, the Boston Red Sox are struggling offensively, averaging just 1.5 runs per game in their last four contests, and are without key outfielder Roman Anthony due to a wrist sprain. The Braves also have a favorable 6-4 head-to-head record against the Red Sox in their last 10 matchups, further solidifying this pick.

picks.game: Los Angeles Dodgers @ Los Angeles Angels
picks.rationale: The Los Angeles Dodgers enter this Freeway Series having won two straight games, while the Los Angeles Angels hold the worst record in MLB at 16-28 and have lost five of their last six. Although Blake Snell (0-1, 12.01 ERA) had a rough 2026 debut, his underlying metrics, including a 119 Stuff+ rating, indicate elite potential, and he is expected to overpower the struggling Angels lineup, which has a meager 78 wRC+ in May. The Angels are also significantly hampered by injuries to key players like catcher Logan O'Hoppe and infielder Anthony Rendon. This combination of strong Dodgers pitching, a struggling Angels offense, and key injuries for the Angels creates a clear edge for the Dodgers to cover the spread.

picks.game: Kansas City Royals @ St. Louis Cardinals
picks.rationale: This pick is strongly supported by a significant pitching mismatch in favor of the Kansas City Royals. Royals starter Michael Wacha boasts an impressive 4-2 record with a 2.65 ERA and a 0.99 WHIP over 51.1 innings, demonstrating excellent control and damage suppression. Conversely, St. Louis Cardinals starter Dustin May has struggled with a 3-4 record, a 4.97 ERA, and a concerning 1.43 WHIP, having allowed 50 hits in just 42.2 innings. Furthermore, the Cardinals are dealing with notable injuries to key position players Ramon Urias and Lars Nootbaar, which will impact their offensive depth. The Royals' pitching advantage and the Cardinals' injuries make the Royals a confident moneyline play.

The Arizona Diamondbacks hold a significant pitching advantage with Merrill Kelly (2-3, 7.53 ERA) on the mound, despite his elevated ERA this season. Kelly is coming off his strongest start, allowing just one run on three hits over seven innings, and has an outstanding career record against the Rockies, going 9-3 with a 3.25 ERA in 17 starts. In contrast, Colorado Rockies starter Kyle Freeland (1-4, 6.01 ERA) has historically struggled against the Diamondbacks, holding a 4-9 record with a 5.94 ERA in 22 career starts. Despite the game being at Coors Field, Kelly's dominant history against Colorado and recent strong performance provide a clear edge for the Diamondbacks.

The Tampa Bay Rays are in excellent form, boasting a 28-14 overall record and an impressive 14-4 record at home this season. They have won 8 of their last 10 games, demonstrating strong recent performance with a collective 2.37 ERA. Rays starter Jesse Scholtens (3-2, 3.34 ERA) will take the mound, backed by a pitching staff that ranks 5th best in MLB with a 3.49 ERA and 2nd best against division opponents with a 2.72 ERA. The Miami Marlins, with Janson Junk (2-3, 3.25 ERA) starting, have a losing road record of 6-12, making the Rays a strong play at home.

picks.game: Minnesota United FC @ New England Revolution
picks.rationale: New England Revolution are in very good form overall with 7 wins, 1 draw, and 3 losses, and boast an excellent home record of 6 wins, 0 draws, and 0 losses. Their recent form shows a strong W-W-D-W-W streak, indicating consistent performance. While Minnesota United FC has a good overall record, their away form is considered very good, but they face a dominant home team in New England. This strong home advantage and superior recent form make the Revolution a high-confidence pick.

Both Philadelphia Union and Columbus Crew SC have shown significant defensive vulnerabilities this season, with the Union conceding 23 goals in 13 games and the Crew shipping 22 goals. This defensive frailty has led to high-scoring matches, as three of Philadelphia's last five and four of Columbus's last five games have gone over 2.5 goals. Furthermore, historical head-to-head data indicates that 56% of their previous encounters have seen over 2.5 goals, with prediction stats for this match also favoring the over at 70%.

picks.game: New York City FC @ New York Red Bulls
picks.rationale: The Hudson River Derby historically produces goals, with the head-to-head average between New York Red Bulls and New York City FC standing at 2.79 goals per match. The most recent encounter on April 29, 2026, saw a 1-3 scoreline, easily clearing the 2.5 goal mark. The New York Red Bulls have a strong tendency for high-scoring home games, with Over 1.5 goals in 90% and Over 2.5 goals in 80% of their home matches this season. New York City FC also contributes to this trend with an overall average of 2.99 goals per game.

Chicago Fire currently holds a stronger position in the MLS table at 4th, compared to CF Montreal at 11th. Head-to-head history also favors Chicago, with 3 wins in the last 6 matches against Montreal's single victory. Crucially, CF Montreal is significantly impacted by injuries, with Fabian Herbers, Owen Graham-Roache, Gennadiy Synchuk, and Bode Hidalgo all unavailable. This combination of better form, H2H advantage, and Montreal's extensive injury list provides a clear edge for Chicago Fire.

picks.game: Atlanta United FC @ Orlando City SC
picks.rationale: Atlanta United FC consistently features in high-scoring games due to their very poor defensive record, having conceded 20 goals in 12 matches. Their matches average 3.23 goals per game, and both teams have scored in 71% of their games. This propensity for goals, even in losses, makes the Over 3.5 an attractive option, especially at plus odds. While specific Orlando City form is not detailed, Atlanta's offensive capabilities and defensive struggles strongly point towards a high-total game.
